rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Insert implementation-level bindings in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes when valid in that scope
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
Ensure that assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports are placed in valid component type scopes
Avoid using subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
anti_patterns: Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Do not use event ports in numeric or Boolean comparisons unless the model provides an explicit data value for the event
